exercises.title: Filter a training log
exercises.prompt: Given a training log file, use a pipe and `grep` to show only the lines containing the loss, and count how many epochs completed. Do it in one command line each.
exercises.hint: `grep loss train.log`; pipe `grep` into `wc -l` to count.
exercises.solution:
```bash
grep -i loss train.log                 # only loss lines
grep -c 'Epoch' train.log              # count epoch lines
tail -f train.log | grep --line-buffered loss   # live-follow just the loss
```

The `tail -f | grep` combo is the everyday move: watch a running job and see only the lines you care about. `--line-buffered` makes grep emit matches immediately instead of buffering.

exercises.title: tmux for long runs
exercises.prompt: Start a tmux session with two panes — one running a (placeholder) training command, one running a GPU/se resource monitor — then detach, and reattach as if you'd closed your laptop and SSH'd back in. Explain why this matters for remote training.
exercises.hint: `tmux new -s train`, split with Ctrl-b %, detach with Ctrl-b d, reattach with `tmux attach -t train`.
exercises.solution:
```bash
tmux new -s train
# in pane 1:
python train.py
# split vertically: Ctrl-b then %
watch -n1 nvidia-smi      # (or htop / nvtop)
# detach: Ctrl-b then d   — training keeps running

tmux attach -t train     # later, after reconnecting over SSH
```

An SSH session that drops kills every foreground process it owns — including an 8-hour training run. tmux keeps the session alive on the server independent of your connection, so you can detach, close the laptop, and reattach later with everything still running.

exercises.title: Sync to a remote box
exercises.prompt: Copy a local project directory to a remote GPU machine efficiently, such that re-running the command only transfers files that changed (not the whole tree), and excludes junk like `.venv` and checkpoints. Explain why `rsync` beats `scp` here.
exercises.hint: `rsync -avz --exclude` over SSH; rerun to see it transfer nothing when unchanged.
exercises.solution:
```bash
rsync -avz --progress \
  --exclude '.venv' --exclude '*.ckpt' --exclude '__pycache__' \
  ./ gpu-box:~/project/
# edit one file, rerun: only that file transfers
```

`scp` re-copies everything every time. `rsync` compares source and destination and sends **only the differences**, so syncing a multi-GB project after a one-line edit takes a second. `-z` compresses in flight, and `--exclude` keeps environments and giant checkpoints off the wire entirely.
